assertions.expectation: Explains that Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support vs 14 months for standard channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'up to 24 months of support (vs 14 months on Regular/Stable)' in the overview section and comparison table

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended channel has additional cost (it's not free)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions 'Extra cost during extended period only (months 15-24)' in the tradeoffs table and includes a detailed cost management section explaining the cost structure

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended channel gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer
assertions.reason: The response doesn't explicitly explain that Extended gets the same versions as Regular but keeps them available longer - it focuses on operational differences without clarifying version availability timing

assertions.expectation: Warns that staying on older versions longer means missing new features and accumulating technical debt
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention missing new features or technical debt accumulation as risks of staying on older versions longer

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel to Extended
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel extended'

assertions.expectation: Advises checking that the current version (1.31) is available on Extended before switching
assertions.reason: Response includes pre-migration checks with commands to verify 1.31 availability in Extended channel and warns about being 'ahead of channel' if not available

assertions.expectation: Recommends Extended for environments with compliance requirements or slow internal change processes
assertions.reason: Response specifically mentions Extended channel is 'ideal for regulated environments (financial services, healthcare)' and recommends it for those needing 'maximum control and extended support windows'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that Extended doesn't mean you can skip upgrades forever — you still must upgrade within 24 months
assertions.reason: Response warns about 'reaching end of extended support' and states clusters will be 'MANUAL ONLY (except at end of extended support)' implying forced upgrades at EoS

assertions.expectation: Notes that minor version upgrades on Extended are NOT automated (except at EoS) — customers must plan and initiate them manually
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'MANUAL ONLY (except at end of extended support)' for minor version auto-upgrades and emphasizes 'You must plan and execute' manual upgrades with detailed operational processes
